3.happening: We create num_map as an empty dictionary.
3.why: The map will remember values we have already passed, along with their indices.
3.invariant: num_map contains only numbers from earlier positions in the scan.

4.happening: We start result as an empty list.
4.why: The answer will be written here once a matching complement is found.
4.invariant: No valid pair has been returned yet.

5.happening: Index i moves to the next array position.
5.why: The algorithm checks each number once from left to right.
5.invariant: Before processing i, num_map contains values from indices less than i.

6.happening: We read the current number nums[i].
6.why: The current number determines what complement we need to reach target.
6.invariant: The current index has not been inserted into num_map yet.

7.happening: We compute complement as target - num.
7.why: If an earlier number equals complement, then that earlier number plus the current number equals target.
7.invariant: A valid pair ending at i must use the value complement from an earlier index.

8.happening: We check whether complement ({complement}) is in the hash map.
8.why: A dictionary lookup tells us in O(1) whether an earlier number can pair with the current one to reach target.
8.invariant: If complement is absent, the current number is saved for later; if present, the answer is ready.

9.happening: We build result using the saved index for complement and the current index i.
9.why: The hash map gives the earlier index in constant time.
9.invariant: result contains two distinct indices whose values add to target.

10.why: The problem guarantees one valid answer, so the scan can stop once it is found.
10.invariant: The returned indices identify two numbers whose sum is target.

11.happening: We insert the current number into num_map with its index.
11.why: A later number may need this value as its complement.
11.invariant: After insertion, num_map represents all processed indices up through i.

init.happening: Initialize the map and result holder.
init.why: The algorithm needs memory of earlier values and a place to store the final answer.
init.invariant: No number has been processed yet.

loop.happening: Advance to the next array index.
loop.why: Scanning once is enough when previous values are stored in a hash map.
loop.invariant: The map contains only values that appeared before the current index.

update.happening: Compute needed values or update stored data.
update.why: Each step either prepares a lookup, records an answer, or saves the current value for later.
update.invariant: The algorithm never pairs an element with itself.

check.happening: Look for the complement in the hash map.
check.why: A dictionary lookup quickly tells us whether a matching earlier value exists.
check.invariant: If the complement is present, its stored index is before i.

return.why: The matching pair has been found.
return.invariant: The returned result satisfies nums[result[0]] + nums[result[1]] == target.
